_doc: Aurelius public-trace benchmark rollup. Read docs/AURELIUS_PUBLIC_TRACE_BENCHMARK_ROLLUP.md and docs/RESULTS.md before using these numbers. Simulator / public-trace results only — directional only, NOT production savings (docs/RESULTS.md §8).
definition_of_safe: for the SLA-safe-headline rollup, only benchmarks where constraint_aware is SAFE (timeout_pct <= 10%, queue_p99 within budget, no SLA-violation regression vs FIFO, no oracle inputs) are counted; UNSAFE baselines (e.g. utilization_aware on Azure 2024 with timeout >10%, robust_energy_standalone on the canonical energy backtest with 143 deadline misses) are NOT used as the headline comparator

vs_naive_baseline.honest_note: the single negative is azure_llm_2023_conv where static do-nothing FIFO at mean-sized capacity is cheaper than CA under mild burst load — reported honestly per docs/RESULTS.md §3

worst_trace.rationale: BurstGPT has only a model-level cache-affinity proxy and CA wins only marginally vs cache_affinity_baseline; +26% vs sla_aware is honestly noted but cache_affinity is the stronger comparator

rollup_llm_serving_only.safety_outcomes: no unsafe regression on any LLM-serving public trace; safety evidence cited per trace in benchmark_inventory.json

rollup_training_gpu_scheduling_only.interpretation: constraint_aware already sits on or near the safe utilization frontier for GPU packing / training scheduling — it WINS only on the price-aware-routing-applicable Alibaba GPU v2023 (+6%); on Philly and MIT Supercloud it TIES the strongest packing baseline (best_fit / FFD / topology_aware / utilization_aware all match within rounding) and WINS big vs naive FIFO (avoids head-of-line blocking).

vs_strongest_realistic_baseline_current_.safety_evidence: constraint_aware deadline_misses=0; current_price_only deadline_misses=0 (parity); robust_energy_standalone 143 misses; greedy_energy 119 misses; sla_aware 143 misses

vs_robust_energy_standalone_unsafe.honest_label: SAFETY_WIN with ~+11% economic alpha; the standalone energy engine has lower raw energy cost but misses 143 deadlines — UNSAFE

static_frontier_controller_vs_constraint.verdict: Azure 2024 is the only applicable trace where the controller produces a measurable goodput/$ uplift over constraint_aware; the others are SAFE_TIEs because constraint_aware already sits at or near the safe-utilization frontier

dynamic_calibration_oracle_alpha_capture.honest_note: Closing the gap requires per-workload pilot calibration, not constant tuning.

rollup_residency_cold_start.small_sample_caveat: n=60 per-request replay; goodput/$ is identical across policies because all share a fixed GPU pool. Diagnostic only.

conservative.headline: Across 8 public-trace and frozen-synthetic backtests, Aurelius improves SLA-safe goodput per infrastructure dollar by a median of ~9% (mean ~19%) vs the strongest realistic safe baselines, with 6 wins / 2 safe ties / 0 unsafe regressions.
must_include_caveats: simulator / public-trace results only — directional only, NOT production savings
strongest realistic safe baseline differs by workload class (sla_aware reactive autoscaler for LLM serving; best_fit packing for GPU scheduling; current_price_only for energy)
ties on training/GPU scheduling are honestly reported — constraint_aware already sits on or near the safe utilization frontier on those workload classes

strong_but_honest_with_up_to.headline: Aurelius shows up to +89% SLA-safe goodput/$ on real LLM-serving traces vs the strongest realistic reactive autoscaler (Alibaba GenAI 2026, primarily a model-affinity / prewarm effect), and +26% on Azure's 44.1M-request 9-day 2024 LLM-inference trace with 21% lower GPU-hours at parity SLA safety; LLM-serving median across 4 traces is +23%, with no unsafe regressions.
must_include_caveats: simulator / public-trace results only — directional only, NOT production savings
"up to" headline must be paired with the median / win-rate so it cannot mislead
Alibaba GenAI gain is attribution-decomposed: ~62% model-affinity/prewarm, ~38% anticipatory sizing
Azure 2024 +26% is mostly utilization/target-rho cost efficiency — not forecasting accuracy

technical_doc.headline: LLM-serving public traces (n=4, 44.1M requests, 9+ days): median +23% SLA-safe goodput/$ vs reactive SLA-aware baselines, with -21% GPU-hours on the largest trace (Azure LLM 2024 week-long) at parity SLA safety. GPU packing / training scheduling traces (n=3, 16.3k jobs, 205+ days): constraint_aware TIES the strongest safe packing baselines (best_fit / FFD / topology_aware) and WINS big (+16% — +62%) vs naive FIFO that suffers head-of-line blocking, suggesting those workloads were already near their safe frontier. Energy / flexible workload (canonical frozen 1000-job CAISO/PJM/ERCOT backtest): +11% SLA-safe goodput/$ vs current_price_only with 0 deadline misses (where robust_energy_standalone is +∼−11% cheaper on energy but misses 143 deadlines — UNSAFE). No unsafe regressions across all 8 traces.
must_include_caveats: all results simulator / public-trace / frozen-synthetic — NOT customer telemetry
production-savings number requires the docs/RESULTS.md §8 production-claim gate to be satisfied
Philly canonical is fixture-scale (full 1 GB LFS not committed); MIT Supercloud is bounded-real (~3 MB sample of ~1-2 TB)
Oracle / clairvoyant baselines are analysis-only and excluded from the headline comparator

what_to_use: the conservative headline above (median +9% across 8 public-trace and frozen-synthetic backtests, with 6/8 wins, 2/8 safe ties, 0 unsafe regressions)
the LLM-serving rollup: median +23% across 4 public LLM-inference traces, with no unsafe regressions
the Azure 2024 result: +26% goodput/$ at -21% GPU-hours, with attribution showing the win is a utilization-target / cost-efficiency effect (not a forecasting effect)
the canonical energy backtest result: +11% goodput/$ at 0 deadline misses vs current_price_only — a SAFETY_WIN with alpha
the cross-trace generalization audit: 100% safe-or-win rate, 0 regressions across the LLM-inference traces that integration was eligible for
what_NOT_to_use: the +89% Alibaba GenAI headline as a stand-alone claim — it is real but workload-specific (model-affinity-heavy stable-diffusion serving) and must be paired with the median
the +457% Alibaba GenAI vs FIFO number as a buyer-facing headline (FIFO is the sanity baseline, not the realistic deployable comparator)
the dynamic frontier estimator's 91% oracle-alpha capture as a production figure (95% target NOT reached; calibration is a pilot-telemetry task)
any number from the residency decision engine small-sample (n=60) per-request replay as economic alpha — it is a diagnostic, not a headline
the robust_energy_standalone's lower energy cost — it has 143 deadline misses (UNSAFE)
the utilization_aware policy on Azure 2024 — it has higher goodput/$ but >10% timeout rate (UNSAFE)
any production-savings phrasing until docs/RESULTS.md §8 gates are satisfied

public_trace_names: Azure LLM Inference Dataset 2024 week-long (DynamoLLM HPCA 2025)
CAISO / PJM / ERCOT energy day-ahead and real-time prices

raw_vs_fixture_status.burstgpt_v1: full raw trace replay (17,689 requests in canonical contiguous window)
raw_vs_fixture_status.azure_llm_2024_week: full raw week-long trace (44.1M rows, 9 days)
raw_vs_fixture_status.alibaba_gpu_v2023: full raw pod list (6,282 jobs, 149 days)
raw_vs_fixture_status.philly_training: FIXTURE-SCALE demonstration (33 jobs; full 1 GB LFS not committed)
raw_vs_fixture_status.mit_supercloud_bounded: bounded REAL S3 sample (10,000 jobs, 55.9 days; ~3 MB downloaded of ~1-2 TB)
raw_vs_fixture_status.canonical_energy_backtest: frozen synthetic 1000-job workload over real CAISO/PJM/ERCOT 2026-02 prices

statistical_coverage.production_readiness_reason: no result here is a production-savings claim. docs/RESULTS.md §8 production-claim gate (customer telemetry, calibrated priors, customer cost basis, shadow-pilot with no SLA regression) is NOT satisfied for any of these traces.

unsafe_baselines_excluded_from_headline: canonical_energy_backtest: sla_aware (143 deadline misses on this trace's warmup-aware scoring)

limitations: Simulator / public-trace results only. NOT production savings (docs/RESULTS.md §8).
No customer telemetry calibration. No customer cost basis. No shadow-pilot validation.
BurstGPT has only model-level cache-affinity proxy (not real KV cache hit rate); Azure 2023/2024 have no cache/session/latency signal at all.
Philly canonical run is fixture-scale; full 1 GB LFS trace is not committed.
MIT Supercloud is a bounded real S3 sample (~3 MB of ~1-2 TB); per-node capacity not published.
Energy backtest uses a synthetic 1000-job workload mix (not customer-derived).
Dynamic frontier oracle-alpha capture aspirational 95% target was NOT reached (final 91.07%).
Residency decision engine (per-request) result is small-sample (n=60) and diagnostic only.
Oracle / clairvoyant baselines are present for analysis but are NEVER used as the headline comparator.
